top_weaknesses.issue: Several lines trip AI-essay detectors: 'The reasons stack', 'The choices are narrow', 'This works until it does not', 'compounds without limit', 'in that order', 'by definition' — pattern of stacked epigrammatic mini-paragraphs
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut three of these standalone refrains; merge the orphan one-liners back into their parent paragraphs

top_weaknesses.issue: 'Compute tax' is the central concept but never operationally defined (per GPU-hour? per kWh? per inference token?) — HN readers will dunk on the gap
top_weaknesses.fix: Add a one-sentence definition under the compute-tax H2 so the policy is concrete before discussing politics

top_weaknesses.issue: Option-1/2/3/4 enumeration is structurally clean but currently prose; on mobile it reads symmetric in a way that pings AI-template
top_weaknesses.fix: Either render as a numbered list with a one-line subhead per option, or break the symmetry by tightening the option-2 and option-4 paragraphs to different shapes

top_weaknesses.issue: Anchor text 'hyperscalers spent up to $660 billion on AI capex in 2026 alone' is a full claim sentence — spammy and hard to scan; '2028 scenario' anchor is non-descriptive
top_weaknesses.fix: Shorten to '$660B hyperscaler AI capex (2026)' and 'Citrini's 2028 fiscal scenario'

top_weaknesses.issue: 'Labor share' is cited in numbers without a parenthetical definition — non-econ readers stall
top_weaknesses.fix: Add a 6-word parenthetical: '(wages and benefits as % of GDP)'

consensus_summary: Strong thesis with a quotable hook ('The math does not break first. Politics does.') and a clean four-beat structure that the SEO-skeptic rates ready. Main fixes: trim the epigrammatic AI-tells, define 'compute tax' operationally, and either listify or de-symmetrize the four options.
